macro_summary: A two-week US-Iran ceasefire announced overnight sent Dow, S&P 500, and Nasdaq futures surging, per Yahoo Finance, with oil prices cratering on hopes the Strait of Hormuz will soon reopen. This is the dominant overnight development — the ceasefire directly challenges the geopolitical premium that has been baked into energy prices and risk assets for five weeks. On the day, SPY closed up 0.18% and QQQ +0.26%, continuing the measured Q1 relief rally, while VIX rose 6.75% to 25.78 — a data-conflict worth noting, as the fast_info source shows +6.7% while history-derived data implies +8.0%; using fast_info as the headline figure. The Fed holds rates at 3.50%-3.75% with the US 10-year yield steady at 4.34%, keeping the higher-for-longer narrative intact ahead of Thursday's CPI print, which is the next major signal reset.
commodities_note: WTI closed at $94.72 (-0.85% on the day per fast_info), but this figure masks a significant data conflict: the history-derived prior close implies WTI fell roughly 16% — likely a data artifact from the ceasefire announcement and futures rollover effects rather than a settled market move. The macro_news digest from earlier in the session referenced WTI near $115, consistent with the week-long Hormuz premium. Treat the $94.72 close with caution and monitor tomorrow's open for a cleaner read on where oil settles post-ceasefire. Gold at $4,862 (+0.67% per fast_info, though history implies a +4.4% move) — the fast_info figure is the headline. The persistent fast_info vs history divergence on Gold also flags a data reliability issue; the direction (higher) is consistent across both sources, suggesting genuine strength rather than artifact. Gold at this level is near all-time highs with no attractive entry — GLD is extended well above its 50-day average, so no action is warranted despite the signal. SOXX rose 1.18% to $347.76 (fast_info; history implies +2.4%), consistent with the semis consolidation narrative. DXY slipped 0.3% to 98.69, a modest USD weakening consistent with ceasefire-driven risk-on sentiment.

C6L_SI.signal_rationale: Signal changed from CAUTION to BUY — Singapore Airlines broke above its 50-day average of SGD 6.69 with above-average volume confirming the move, and the overnight US-Iran ceasefire announcement is a direct fundamental catalyst: Middle East airspace closures have been a material operating headwind for five weeks, disrupting the Europe-Asia corridor and forcing costly reroutes via Turkey and Egypt. A Hormuz reopening removes that structural drag on fuel costs and route economics simultaneously. Invalidation at SGD 6.69 (the 50-day average, 1.5% below current price); tight stop — normal intraday volatility could test this level, so use the structural support at SGD 6.56 (3.4% below) as the mental stop for position sizing. R:R to first resistance at SGD 7.20 is 4.0:1, which is favourable. Valuation is reasonable: forward PE of 18.6x carries a 33.8% premium to the Singapore cluster median of 13.9x, but the 8.08% FCF yield and 5.32% dividend yield provide meaningful downside cushion. The key concern is the analyst consensus flagging a 64.3% earnings decline — a material headwind. Per Reuters, Air India's CEO Campbell Wilson resigned amid persistent losses, which is a minor read-through suggesting regional aviation stress but does not directly affect SIA's core business. Revenue growth of just 1.9% at a 33.8% PE premium to peers requires the airspace normalisation catalyst to actually deliver margin recovery — if the ceasefire fails or Hormuz remains disrupted, this setup collapses back to CAUTION. Bottom line: SIA is a reasonable price for what you're getting if the ceasefire holds — the airspace reopening story is real and directly improves unit economics. The binary risk is the ceasefire durability; enter with a half position and stop at SGD 6.56.

Z74_SI.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at WATCH. Singapore Telecommunications sits 1.27% above its 50-day average of SGD 4.93 with RSI at 50.5 — technically clean and within the entry zone. The tight stop at SGD 4.93 (1.2% below, tight_invalidation flagged) means normal intraday moves could trigger invalidation before the thesis is tested; use the structural support at SGD 4.43 (11.2% below) as the mental stop for position sizing. Would enter on a pullback to SGD 4.90-4.95 on rising volume, or on a volume-confirmed break above SGD 5.15 resistance.

NVDA.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. NVIDIA's R:R is severely distorted: the nearest support at $177.41 is only 0.4% below current price — a mathematically meaningless stop that normal intraday volatility will test daily. Use the structural support at $169.81 (4.7% below) as the actual thesis test level. Above-SMA50 the picture is clearer: NVDA at $178.10 sits 2.41% below its 50-day average of $182.50, which is declining — confirming the near-term downtrend. RSI at 49.4 is neutral-bearish. Volume is below average. The Blackwell Ultra ramp thesis (per NVDA Q4 FY2026 earnings, $78B Q1 guide) remains intact, and at 16x forward PE — 22% below the semis cluster median — NVDA is not expensive relative to peers given 73.2% revenue growth and 95.6% analyst-consensus earnings growth. The TurboQuant algorithm displacement threat (Day 13 from Google's March 27 announcement) remains the latent structural risk: unvalidated but credibility at risk heading into Q1 earnings season. A Motley Fool piece speculating NVDA worth more than MSFT, GOOGL, and PLTR combined by 2030 is an editorial opinion, not a verified claim. CAUTION source is base_scorer — no hard block. Bottom line: NVDA is genuinely cheap relative to growth, but the chart is not yet cooperating and the TurboQuant overhang hasn't cleared. Wait for a reclaim of the 50-day average at $182.50 with volume before acting.

AMD.signal_rationale: Signal changed from WATCH to CAUTION — AMD has surged 9.67% in five days to sit exactly 5.01% above its 50-day average of $210.97, triggering the hard block. The SMA50 is declining, days above it are just 2, and RSI at 59 meets the >55 threshold that prevents a trend exception. The framework prohibits entry at this extension without a sustained structural trend. The AMD-Meta GPU supply framework (per AMD IR press release, February 24, 2026) for MI450 Instinct deployments — up to 6GW capacity with performance warrants — remains the core multi-year thesis, but this is a framework ceiling, not a firm purchase order; do not treat the dollar ceiling figure as committed revenue. Revenue from first 1GW shipments begins H2 2026 with full 6GW by 2030 contingent on MI450 meeting benchmarks. The hyperscaler shift toward Arm-based chips for custom AI ASICs (per Simply Wall St.) is a real demand headwind for AMD's x86 data center CPU franchise — worth monitoring as it could clip the server CPU upside that analysts are modeling. At 20.5x forward PE with PEG 0.65 and 34.1% revenue growth, AMD is attractively valued relative to the semis cluster median, and the 217.1% expected earnings growth is striking. Invalidation at $210.97 (50-day SMA, 4.8% below); structural support at $197.35 (10.9% below) is the real thesis test. R:R to nearest resistance at $225.98 is only 0.4:1 from current price — unfavourable. Bottom line: AMD's thesis is intact and valuation is compelling, but you're being asked to buy into a momentum chase 5% above the average. Wait for a pullback to $212-215 before acting.

INTC.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at HOLD. Intel's 22.68% five-day surge — driven in part by the confirmed Terafab partnership with Elon Musk's Tesla, SpaceX, and xAI for a chip design facility (per Yahoo Finance) — has pushed INTC to 14.8% above its declining 50-day average of $46.09, a hard block with no trend exception possible at this extension. The 18A foundry thesis (per Intel investor day and Q4 2025 earnings) continues to advance — yield improvements are confirmed — but no external customers are publicly committed; internal products only (Panther Lake). Do not cite 18A as a confirmed positive catalyst until external wins are announced. The Terafab news is a new, early-stage development that could generate future foundry demand, but the commercial structure and timeline are unspecified. At 52.7x forward PE — 157% above the semis cluster median of 20.5x — with negative revenue growth (-4.1%) and negative FCF yield (-1.7%), INTC's valuation is difficult to justify on current fundamentals. Earnings April 24 (16 days out) is the key event: confirmation of foundry progress and any external customer hints could validate a signal upgrade to WATCH. Invalidation at the 50-day SMA of $46.09 (12.9% below); structural support at $42.58 (19.5% below). R:R to first resistance at $54.60 is only 0.2:1 — unfavourable from current levels. Bottom line: Intel is expensive for what it's delivering today; the story is about what it might become. Wait for the April 24 earnings print and a pullback closer to $46-48 before reconsidering entry.

ASML.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. ASML at $1,306.45 sits 6.19% below its 50-day average of $1,392.66, which is rising — a divergent setup where the price is recovering but hasn't reclaimed the trend. Invalidation at the nearest support of $1,283.60 (1.7% below) — tight enough that the writeup must flag both levels: structural support at $1,052.84 (19.4% below) is the real thesis test if the CAUTION persists. R:R to first resistance at $1,410.41 is 4.7:1, which is technically favourable, but caution_source is base_scorer, meaning the CAUTION is fundamentally driven. Per GuruFocus, a new US bipartisan MATCH Act could block DUV equipment sales and servicing to major Chinese chipmakers — a direct incremental threat to ASML's installed-base business in China, which has been a resilience pillar. ASML's $71B revenue by 2030 target (per company guidance) assumes Chinese demand access that this legislation would restrict. At 29.8x forward PE with PEG 2.18 — above the 2.0 threshold — and revenue growth of only 4.9%, the multiple is expensive for the growth rate. Earnings April 15 (7 days out) — a HIGH impact binary event that could resolve the CAUTION: strong order intake or guidance holding despite MATCH Act risk would be a catalyst to upgrade to WATCH; a guidance cut would deepen the CAUTION. Bottom line: ASML is the best lithography business in the world, but you're paying a premium multiple at a moment when China revenue is under legislative threat. Wait for the April 15 earnings print before acting.

MU.signal_rationale: Signal changed from ACCUMULATE (prior session) to CAUTION — MU's ACCUMULATE gate has been re-evaluated and the rr_distorted flag has been checked: rr_distorted is false, ratio is 4.3:1, rr_quality is observed, caution_source is base_scorer, and VIX is below 30. However, MU's FCF yield is 0.68% (below the 2% gate required for ACCUMULATE), blocking the upgrade. The valuation data shows anomalous metrics: PEG of 0.21 with forward PE of 3.8x against trailing PE of 17.8x implies a massive expected earnings inflection — reported as 756% earnings growth — treat this as extraordinary and verify rather than citing with conviction. The dividend yield field shows 16.0%, which is almost certainly a data artifact (likely trailing dividend divided by current price post-decline) and should not be cited as a yield. Revenue growth of 196.3% reflects the HBM demand surge. MU is acquiring Powerchip's Taichung P5 site to expand DRAM capacity (per Simply Wall St.) — a structural capacity investment aligned with the AI memory thesis. The HBM demand story (inference-driven KV cache scaling, not training) is intact. MU's CEO projections about memory price increases carry inherent seller bias and should be flagged accordingly. Invalidation at nearest support $363.75 (3.7% below); R:R to first resistance at $437.65 is 4.3:1 — favourable ratio, but FCF yield gate blocks ACCUMULATE. Structural support at $357.52 (5.3% below) is the next meaningful level. Bottom line: MU is genuinely cheap if the earnings inflection delivers, but it's not generating free cash flow yet — the thesis depends entirely on the HBM ramp. Watch for a volume-confirmed hold of $357-364 support before acting.

AMZN.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at HOLD. Amazon at $213.77 is 0.37% below its 50-day average of $214.57 — essentially sitting on the average with RSI at 52.8 in the neutral zone. The confirmed Amazon-USPS delivery partnership (per Simply Wall St.) to handle over 1 billion packages annually reshapes the logistics competitive dynamic, reducing third-party carrier dependency — a margin-positive structural development. However, the $200B AI capex commitment (per AMZN Q4 2025 earnings call and 10-K filing) continues to weigh on near-term earnings; the Q1 operating income miss that drove the prior selloff is still fresh. FCF yield at 1.04% is thin, meaning the thesis depends on future AWS growth translating to earnings. Forward PE at 22.8x is in line with the BigTech cluster median. Invalidation at nearest support $211.22 (1.2% below) — tight; structural support at $197.66 (7.5% below) is the actual thesis test. R:R at 2.2:1 to $219.43 resistance is modestly favourable but volume is below average. Bottom line: AMZN is fairly priced at the current level with a neutral technical setup. Upgrades to WATCH on a volume-confirmed reclaim above $214.57 with the SMA50 flattening; reassess below $211.

GOOG.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Google's 11% five-day surge has triggered the momentum chase block (>10% five-day move), making this an entry-blocked CAUTION regardless of the underlying setup. At $303.93, GOOG sits 1.76% below its declining 50-day average of $309.38 — close to reclaiming it, but the entry block stands. PEG of 2.21 at this valuation is above the 2.0 threshold, flagging expensive-for-growth pricing. Revenue growth of 18% against 22.6x forward PE is reasonable but not compelling enough to override the entry discipline. The Google-Broadcom expanded chip deal (per Yahoo Finance) and TPU v7 datacenter dominance thesis (Google Cloud Next 2025) remain intact. The quantum computing warning about crypto security risks (per Simply Wall St.) is a long-duration narrative irrelevant to near-term price action. TurboQuant (Day 13) remains a latent structural risk to the GPU demand narrative. Earnings April 30 (22 days out) is the next key catalyst — Q1 cloud revenue and TPU adoption will be the thesis validators. Invalidation at $295.95 (2.6% below); structural support at $265.77 (12.6% below). R:R 0.9:1 to $311.07 — unfavourable. Bottom line: Google's AI infrastructure thesis is credible, but you're buying after an 11% five-day run with momentum already captured. Wait for the surge to consolidate and the SMA50 to be reclaimed cleanly with volume.

COHR.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at WATCH. The overnight US-Iran ceasefire announcement is a direct negative catalyst for XLE — the Hormuz-driven energy premium that pushed oil above $115 this week is now at risk of unwinding rapidly. XLE at $60.16 sits 8.3% above its rising 50-day average, hard-blocked. Avoid entry; reassess if ceasefire collapses and WTI reclaims $105+, which would signal the Hormuz disruption is structural.

PLTR.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at WATCH. Palantir at $150.07 sits 2.4% above its declining 50-day average of $146.55, within the entry zone (within 2%) with RSI at 49.0 — technically approaching an entry condition. The defense AI contract momentum thesis (Day 20) remains structurally intact: the $10B Army IDIQ framework ceiling and $448M Navy ShipOS deal (per DoD press release) reflect demand that is insulated from commercial hyperscaler capex uncertainty. Revenue guide of $7.18-7.20B (+61% YoY per company guidance) is the quantitative anchor. However, forward PE of 80.6x — 253.5% above the BigTech cluster median of 22.8x — is a significant valuation burden. The soft pipeline block (>40x forward PE) requires explicit justification: 70% revenue growth and 647% expected earnings growth (analyst consensus) provide a partial rationale, but the PEG of 2.94 says the market is already pricing in considerable optimism. Invalidation at the 50-day SMA of $146.55 (2.3% below); structural support at $119.17 (20.6% below). R:R to first resistance at $161.92 is 3.4:1 — favourable if the entry is near $148-150. Earnings May 5 (27 days out) is the key validation event. The Iran ceasefire is a modest negative for PLTR's near-term defense contract narrative. Bottom line: PLTR is expensive but growing fast — it makes sense for patient investors if you believe defense AI budgets expand regardless of the Iran situation. Actionable on a pullback to $147-149 (near SMA50) with volume confirmation.

singapore.summary: The cluster's dominant overnight development is the US-Iran ceasefire — directly positive for Singapore Airlines (C6L_SI upgraded to BUY on airspace normalisation) and modestly negative for banks via oil-price deflation reducing inflation expectations. The NIM compression thesis for DBS, OCBC, and UOB remains intact with US10Y at 4.34%, well above the 4.20% reset threshold. The MAS Monetary Policy Statement on April 14 is the next cluster-level catalyst.
singapore.thesis_status: Mixed — C6L breakout, banks structurally constrained
singapore.key_development: US-Iran ceasefire announcement directly removes the Europe-Asia airspace disruption premium that has been C6L's key headwind for five weeks.

semiconductors.summary: SOXX at $347.76 (+1.18% per fast_info) continues its recovery, now approximately 16% below the pre-TurboQuant peak of $415.47 — the algorithm displacement threat (Day 13) remains unresolved heading into a dense earnings season. ASML earnings on April 15 is the first major data point; MATCH Act legislative risk is a new incremental headwind for the cluster's China revenue exposure. Within the cluster, MU's capacity expansion into Taiwan is the most concrete fundamental development, while INTC's Terafab partnership is early-stage and unquantified.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Recovery fragile — TurboQuant overhang and MATCH Act risk
semiconductors.key_development: Bipartisan MATCH Act targeting DUV sales to Chinese chipmakers (per GuruFocus) adds a new legislative dimension to the US-China semiconductor supply chain reshaping narrative — ASML most directly exposed.

big_tech.summary: The cluster is technically mixed: AMZN sits on its 50-day average (HOLD), GOOG is recovering but momentum-blocked after an 11% five-day surge (CAUTION), and PLTR remains the highest-conviction setup at 2.4% above its 50-day average. The ceasefire is marginally negative for PLTR's defense narrative but not thesis-breaking — defense budgets don't reverse on a ceasefire of unspecified durability.
big_tech.thesis_status: Mixed — PLTR best positioned, GOOG and AMZN waiting for clarity
big_tech.key_development: Amazon-USPS confirmed 1B+ package delivery partnership reshapes the logistics competitive picture and is a margin-positive structural development for AWS's logistics subsidiary.

photonics.summary: LITE remains in extreme extension territory (32% above SMA50, hard-blocked) while COHR is recoverable at 5.7% above its SMA50 — a pullback to $245-250 would make COHR actionable. Both tickers are backed by confirmed NVDA $2B investments (NVIDIA newsroom, March 2, 2026), making the structural thesis sound. The divergence within the cluster is entirely a function of S&P 500 inclusion timing and post-inclusion momentum.
photonics.thesis_status: Structurally intact — LITE too extended, COHR near entry
photonics.key_development: No new photonics-specific catalyst today; the NVDA investment and S&P 500 inclusion remain the structural anchors for both names.

The 67-session trend above the SMA50 was geopolitical in origin and has not converted to structural. Energy stocks were modestly higher on the day (per MT Newswires) before the ceasefire news hit, meaning tomorrow's open is likely to see pressure on the sector.

energy.key_development: US-Iran two-week ceasefire announced overnight, per Yahoo Finance — Hormuz reopening expectations will crater the oil premium that drove XLE's move.

VIX at 25.78 (elevated) suggests institutional hedging remains active despite the Q1 relief rally.

index.key_development: Overnight S&P 500 futures surge on ceasefire news provides the first meaningful catalyst for a potential VUAA_L signal upgrade if sustained with volume.

MATCH Act bipartisan US bill targeting DUV equipment sales and servicing to major Chinese chipmakers — direct ASML China revenue risk
Middle East airspace closures disrupting Europe-Asia aviation corridor — partially resolved by ceasefire but operational restoration takes days to weeks
Fed higher-for-longer rate stance with US10Y at 4.34% — structural headwind to tech multiples and Singapore bank NIM recovery
geopolitical.market_impact: The ceasefire announcement is the dominant overnight signal — WTI's data-conflicted close near $94.72 (fast_info) versus the $115+ level from the week suggests oil is already repricing the de-escalation, which directly pressures XLE and removes C6L_SI's key headwind. The MATCH Act adds legislative uncertainty to ASML's China revenue assumption, keeping the semis cluster in CAUTION despite SOXX's 1.18% daily gain.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: US-Iran two-week ceasefire announced overnight per Yahoo Finance, sending futures surging and oil prices lower — the single largest geopolitical development in five weeks of the Hormuz crisis.
geopolitical.portfolio_action: Selective entry on ceasefire beneficiaries (C6L.SI), hold cash on energy (XLE), and wait for ASML April 15 earnings before acting on semis — ceasefire durability is unproven and two weeks is a short window.
